title: Getting started with zeitR
text: zeitR R package importing, parsing, analysing raw actigraphy recordings wrist-worn devices. reads device output files, runs full rest-activity pipeline, returns tidy data frames ready chronobiological analysis. zeitR part Circadia Lab R ecosystem:

title: Getting started with zeitR
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("circadia-bio/zeitR")

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: main entry point read_actigraphy(). accepts path raw device file returns zeitr_recording object two slots: $epochs (epoch-level tibble) $metadata (device subject information file header).
code: rec <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") rec ## zeitr_recording: P001 ## * Device:   ActTrust2 (ID 828) ## * Firmware: 1.4 ## * Interval: 60 s ## * Epochs:   10080 ## * From:     2021-05-27 11:10:15 ## * To:       2021-06-03 11:09:15

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: row one epoch (60 seconds default). key columns :

title: Getting started with zeitR
code: rec$metadata ## $subject ## [1] "Julius" ## $device_model ## [1] "ActTrust2" ## $interval_s ## [1] 60 ## ...

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: run_pipeline() chains analysis steps one call: timestamp consistency check → prepare → -wrist detection → sleep period detection (Crespo) → nap detection (Crespo) → WASO computation (Cole-Kripke).
code: result <- run_pipeline("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") result ## zeitr_result: P001 ## * Source:  .../recordings/P001.txt ## * Epochs:  10080 ## * Nights:  7 ## * Issues:  0

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: metrics : Tip: Multiply epoch counts result$data$metadata$interval_s / 60 convert minutes.
code: result$nights ## # A tibble: 7 x 11 ##   night is_nap bed_time            get_up_time          tbt   tst  waso   sol   soi    nw   eff ##   <int> <lgl>  <dttm>              <dttm>             <int> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <int> <int> <dbl> ## 1     1 FALSE  2021-05-27 22:04:15 2021-05-28 07:18:15  494   432    48    12     2    18  0.874 ## ...

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: issues found, zero-row tibble. Issues flagged gaps > 120 s, backward jumps, year artefacts (1970 2000).
code: result$issues

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: control, step can called separately. useful want inspect intermediate outputs customise algorithm parameters.
code: # 1. Read rec  <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo")  # 2. Check timestamps issues <- check_consistency(rec$epochs)  # 3. Prepare (temperature clamping, state columns) prep <- prepare_actigraphy(rec$epochs)  # 4. Off-wrist detection (Condor bimodal algorithm) prep <- detect_offwrist_bimodal(prep) sum(prep$state == 4)  # off-wrist epochs  # 5. Main sleep periods (Crespo, 2012) prep <- detect_sleep_crespo(prep)  # 6. Naps (Crespo, 2012) prep <- detect_naps_crespo(prep)  # 7. WASO + nightly stats (Cole-Kripke, 1992) out <- compute_waso(prep) out$nights

title: Getting started with zeitR
text: vignette("npcra") — computing , IV, RA, L5, M10 recording vignette("study-analysis") — batch reading participant-level summaries ?run_pipeline — full documentation pipeline parameters ?detect_sleep_crespo — Crespo algorithm parameters ?score_epochs_cole_kripke — Cole-Kripke scoring details

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) characterises 24-hour rest-activity pattern without assuming sinusoidal waveform. introduced Van Someren et al. (1997, 1999) become standard approach actigraphy-based circadian assessment healthy clinical populations. zeitR implements compute_npcra(), returns five core variables single function call.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: quantifies consistent 24-hour activity pattern across days. computed ratio variance average hourly activity profile overall variance raw signal: =np⋅∑h=1p(x‾h−x‾)2∑=1n(xi−x‾)2 \text{} = \frac{n}{p} \cdot \frac{\sum_{h=1}^{p} \left( \bar{x}_h - \bar{x} \right)^2}      {\sum_{=1}^{n} \left( x_i   - \bar{x} \right)^2} nn total number epochs, pp number epochs per day, x‾h\bar{x}_h mean activity hour--day hh, x‾\bar{x} grand mean. Range: 0–1. Higher values indicate stable, clock-driven rhythm. Typical values healthy adults 0.6–0.8.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: IV measures fragmentation rest-activity rhythm — often abruptly signal transitions rest activity: IV=n∑=2n(xi−xi−1)2(n−1)∑=1n(xi−x‾)2 \text{IV} = \frac{n \sum_{=2}^{n} (x_i - x_{-1})^2}                  {(n-1) \sum_{=1}^{n} (x_i - \bar{x})^2} Range: ≥0\geq 0. Higher values indicate fragmented rhythm. Values 1.0 often seen older adults patients neurological disorders.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: RA captures contrast active least active periods day: RA=M10−L5M10+L5 \text{RA} = \frac{M10 - L5}{M10 + L5} Range: 0–1. Higher values indicate stronger amplitude circadian rhythm.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: L5 mean activity level least active 5-hour window 24-hour averaged profile. M10 mean activity active 10-hour window. computed 24-hour average profile (raw epochs), making robust day--day variability. onset times (L5_onset, M10_onset) give start time respective windows HH:MM format.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: compute_npcra() accepts either zeitr_recording (read_actigraphy()) data frame datetime activity columns.
code: rec   <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") npcra <- compute_npcra(rec) npcra ## # A tibble: 1 x 10 ##   participant_id    IS    IV    RA    L5 L5_onset   M10 M10_onset n_days n_epochs ##   <chr>          <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>    <dbl> <chr>      <dbl>    <int> ## 1 P001            0.72  0.43  0.89  12.3 02:30    84.7  11:00       7.0     10080

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: participant P001: = 0.72 — moderately stable rhythm, consistent healthy adult regular working hours. IV = 0.43 — low fragmentation; rest-activity transitions relatively smooth. RA = 0.89 — strong circadian amplitude; large contrast active rest phases. L5 onset = 02:30 — least active window centred around 02:30, expected typical sleeper. M10 onset = 11:00 — active window peaks mid-morning.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: 5-hour 10-hour windows defaults. can adjusted: Note IV independent window parameters; L5, M10, RA affected.
code: # Narrower windows — more sensitive to sharp peaks compute_npcra(rec, L5_hours = 4, M10_hours = 8)

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: activity data plain data frame (e.g. imported another tool), compute_npcra() works directly: epoch_s argument optional — omitted estimated median inter-epoch interval datetime.
code: df <- data.frame(   datetime = seq(     as.POSIXct("2021-05-27 00:00:00", tz = "UTC"),     by = 60,     length.out = 10080   ),   activity = c(...)  # your activity counts )  compute_npcra(df, epoch_s = 60)

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: multiple participants, study_summary() calls compute_npcra() every recording zeitr_study returns single summary tibble. See vignette("study-analysis") full walkthrough.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis
text: Van Someren, E. J. W., Lijzenga, C., Mirmiran, M., & Swaab, D. F. (1997). Long-term fitness training improves circadian rest-activity rhythm healthy elderly males. Journal Biological Rhythms, 12(2), 146–156. https://doi.org/10.1177/074873049701200206 Van Someren, E. J. W., Swaab, D. F., Colenda, C. C., Cohen, W., McCall, W. V., & Rosenquist, P. B. (1999). Bright light therapy: Improved sensitivity effects rest-activity rhythms Alzheimer patients application nonparametric methods. Chronobiology International, 16(4), 505–518. https://doi.org/10.3109/07420529908998724 Marler, M. R., Gehrman, P., Martin, J. L., & Ancoli-Israel, S. (2006). sigmoidally transformed cosine curve: mathematical model circadian rhythms symmetric non-sinusoidal shapes. Statistics Medicine, 25(22), 3893–3904. https://doi.org/10.1002/sim.2466

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
text: read_acttrust() parses raw ActTrust file returns zeitr_recording two slots: $epochs (epoch-level tibble) $metadata (device subject information file header). recording spans 52 days 1-minute epochs — typical ambulatory study length.

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
text: run_pipeline() chains analysis steps sequence: Timestamp consistency check Prepare (temperature clamping, state column initialisation) -wrist detection (Condor bimodal algorithm) Main sleep period detection (Crespo, 2012) Nap detection (Crespo, 2012) WASO scoring (Cole-Kripke, 1992)
code: result <- run_pipeline(FILE, tz = TZ, quiet = TRUE) #> ℹ Reading input1.txt ... #> ✔ [input1] Done. 55 night(s) detected. result #>  #> ── zeitr_result: input1 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Source: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/zeitR/extdata/input1.txt #> • Epochs: 76196 #> • Nights: 55 #> • Issues: 5

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
text: result$nights contains one row per detected sleep period (main nights naps). Epoch counts minutes 1-minute epoch device. Key sleep variables:
code: result$nights |>   mutate(     bed_time    = format(bed_time,    "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),     get_up_time = format(get_up_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),     tbt_h       = round(tbt  / 60, 1),     tst_h       = round(tst  / 60, 1),     eff_pct     = round(eff  * 100,  1)   ) |>   select(night, is_nap, bed_time, get_up_time, tbt_h, tst_h, waso, sol, eff_pct) |>   knitr::kable(     col.names = c("Night", "Nap?", "Bed time", "Get-up time",                   "TBT (h)", "TST (h)", "WASO (min)", "SOL (min)", "Eff (%)"),     align = "clllrrrrr"   )

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
code: if (nrow(result$issues) == 0L) {   cat("No timestamp issues detected.\n") } else {   knitr::kable(result$issues) }

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
text: actogram displays full epoch-level state sequence raster, one row per day time--day x-axis. standard visual summary actigraphy data.  recording shows consistent nocturnal sleep pattern (dark purple bands centred around midnight) brief -wrist episode visible first week.
code: state_colours <- c(   "wake"      = "#D9C8A0",   "sleep"     = "#3B2F6B",   "off-wrist" = "#C25E2A" )  d <- result$data |>   mutate(     state_label         = label_states(state),     date                = as.Date(datetime, tz = TZ),     mins_since_midnight = as.integer(format(datetime, "%H")) * 60L +                           as.integer(format(datetime, "%M"))   )  ggplot(d, aes(x = mins_since_midnight,               y = forcats::fct_rev(factor(date)),               fill = state_label)) +   geom_tile(height = 0.9, width = 1) +   scale_fill_manual(values = state_colours, name = "State", drop = TRUE) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = seq(0, 23 * 60, 4 * 60),     labels = function(x) sprintf("%02d:00", x %/% 60L),     expand = c(0, 0),     limits = c(0, 24 * 60)   ) +   scale_y_discrete(     breaks = function(x) x[seq(1, length(x), by = 7)],     labels = function(x) format(as.Date(x), "%d %b"),     expand = c(0.01, 0.01)   ) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = paste0("Actogram \u2014 ", result$subject_id)) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(     panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3),     panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),     legend.position    = "bottom",     legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),     plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),     axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10)   ) #> Warning: Removed 53 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_tile()`).

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
text: compute_npcra() derives standard non-parametric circadian rhythm variables raw activity time series. healthy adult regular sleep-wake schedule expect: > 0.6 (consistent 24 h rhythm) IV < 1.0 (low fragmentation) RA > 0.8 (high contrast rest activity) L5 onset around 01:00–04:00 (nocturnal rest trough) M10 onset around 08:00–12:00 (morning activity peak)
code: npcra <- compute_npcra(rec)  npcra |>   select(-participant_id) |>   mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") |>   mutate(Description = c(     "Interdaily stability (0\u20131; higher = more consistent rhythm)",     "Intradaily variability (\u22650; higher = more fragmented rhythm)",     "Relative amplitude (0\u20131; contrast between M10 and L5)",     "Mean activity during the least-active 5 h window",     "Clock time of the L5 window onset (hh:mm)",     "Mean activity during the most-active 10 h window",     "Clock time of the M10 window onset (hh:mm)",     "Recording duration in days",     "Total number of epochs"   )) |>   knitr::kable(col.names = c("Variable", "Value", "Description"),                align = "lrl")

title: Single-recording sleep analysis
text: vignette("study-analysis") — batch processing across multiple participants vignette("npcra") — NPCRA variable definitions interpretation ?run_pipeline — full pipeline parameter reference ?acttrust_params — default algorithm parameters ActTrust device

title: Study-level analysis
text: study contains multiple participants, zeitR provides two functions work group level: read_actigraphy_dir() — reads actigraphy files folder zeitr_study object. study_summary() — computes per-participant NPCRA variables recording quality metrics across whole study.

title: Study-level analysis
text: Place recording files (one per participant) single folder. filename stem used participant ID. zeitr_study named list zeitr_recording objects. Individual recordings accessed participant ID:
code: study <- read_actigraphy_dir(   "recordings/",   device  = "acttrust",   pattern = "*.txt",   tz      = "America/Sao_Paulo" ) study ## zeitr_study: 12 recording(s) ## * P001: 10080 epochs ## * P002:  9950 epochs ## * P003: 10056 epochs ## ... study[["P001"]]$epochs study[["P001"]]$metadata

title: Study-level analysis
text: study_summary() iterates every recording study, calls compute_npcra() participant, returns single summary tibble:

what: Running the full pipeline across a study
title: Study-level analysis
text: run_pipeline_batch() runs complete pipeline (-wrist detection, Crespo sleep detection, Cole-Kripke WASO) every file folder: element results zeitr_result (see vignette("getting-started")). extract nightly statistics single data frame:
code: results <- run_pipeline_batch(   "recordings/",   pattern = "*.txt",   tz      = "America/Sao_Paulo" ) library(dplyr)  all_nights <- lapply(names(results), function(pid) {   df <- results[[pid]]$nights   df$participant_id <- pid   df }) |>   bind_rows()  all_nights

title: Study-level analysis
text: common workflow combine group-level NPCRA study_summary() mean nightly statistics run_pipeline_batch():
code: # 1. NPCRA summary npcra_df <- study_summary(study)  # 2. Mean nightly stats per participant sleep_df <- lapply(names(results), function(pid) {   nts <- results[[pid]]$nights   data.frame(     participant_id = pid,     mean_tst_min   = mean(nts$tst, na.rm = TRUE) * 60 / 60,  # epochs to min     mean_eff       = mean(nts$eff, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_waso_min  = mean(nts$waso, na.rm = TRUE),     n_nights       = nrow(nts)   ) }) |>   bind_rows()  # 3. Join full_df <- left_join(npcra_df, sleep_df, by = "participant_id") full_df

title: Study-level analysis
text: Skipped files: read_actigraphy_dir() run_pipeline_batch() skip files fail parse emit warning, single corrupt file abort whole study run. Time zones: Set tz local recording time zone correct circadian alignment (especially L5/M10 onset times). Using "UTC" recording made another time zone shift circadian onset times UTC offset. Epoch duration: zeitR estimates epoch duration automatically median inter-epoch interval. files inconsistent epoch lengths, pass epoch_s explicitly study_summary() compute_npcra().

title: Study-level analysis
text: vignette("getting-started") — single-file pipeline walkthrough vignette("npcra") — NPCRA variable definitions interpretation ?read_actigraphy_dir, ?study_summary, ?run_pipeline_batch

what: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: Actigraphy data parsing analysis R.  ⚠️ zeitR early development formally validated. pipeline validated epoch--epoch Condor circadiaBase Python reference ActTrust recording, package undergone formal peer review. Verify outputs independently using research context.

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: zeitR R package importing, parsing, analysing raw actigraphy recordings wrist-worn devices. runs full rest-activity pipeline — -wrist detection, sleep period identification, WASO computation — computes standard non-parametric circadian rhythm variables (, IV, RA, L5, M10), returning tidy data frames ready downstream chronobiological analysis. zeitR designed complement slumbR Circadia Lab ecosystem: slumbR handles sleep diary questionnaire data, zeitR handles actigraphy side study, speak tidy, pipeline-friendly R idioms.

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: 📥 read_actigraphy() — parse raw device file zeitr_recording object $epochs $metadata 📂 read_actigraphy_dir() — batch-read whole directory files zeitr_study 🔍 check_consistency() — flag timestamp gaps, backward jumps, firmware year artefacts 🦾 detect_offwrist_bimodal() — Condor bimodal activity/temperature -wrist detection (three-stage refiner) 😴 detect_sleep_crespo() — main sleep period detection (Crespo et al., 2012) 💤 detect_naps_crespo() — secondary sleep period (nap) detection (Crespo et al., 2012) ⏱️ score_epochs_cole_kripke() — epoch-level wake/sleep scoring (Cole & Kripke, 1992) 📊 compute_waso() — nightly TBT, TST, WASO, SOL, SOI, number awakenings, sleep efficiency 📐 compute_npcra() — non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis: , IV, RA, L5, M10 🗂️ study_summary() — participant-level NPCRA summary across whole study 🚀 run_pipeline() — run complete pipeline single file one call 🗃️ run_pipeline_batch() — run complete pipeline across directory files 🏷️ label_states() — convert integer epoch states human-readable factor ("wake", "sleep", "nap", "-wrist") ⚙️ acttrust_params() — device parameter preset; copy modify adapt pipeline devices

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("circadia-bio/zeitR")

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: library(zeitR)  rec <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") rec$epochs    # tidy epoch-level tibble rec$metadata  # device info, firmware, epoch length

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: result <- run_pipeline("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo")  result$nights  # nightly sleep statistics result$data    # epoch-level tibble with state, sleep, offwrist columns result$issues  # timestamp consistency issues (0 rows if none)

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: # Convert integer states to readable labels for display or plotting result$data$state_label <- label_states(result$data$state)  table(result$data$state_label) #>      wake     sleep       nap off-wrist #>     48231     24603       892      2470

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: # The pipeline ships with ActTrust-validated defaults via acttrust_params(). # To adapt for a different device, copy and modify: p <- acttrust_params() p$sleep$sleep_quantile <- 1/3   # original Crespo (2012) threshold  result <- run_pipeline("recordings/P001.txt", params = p)

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: npcra <- compute_npcra(rec) npcra #>   participant_id    IS    IV    RA    L5 L5_onset   M10 M10_onset n_days n_epochs #>            P001  0.72  0.43  0.89  12.3    02:30  84.7     11:00    7.0    10080

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: study <- read_actigraphy_dir("recordings/", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") study_summary(study) #>   participant_id n_epochs n_days    IS    IV    RA    L5 L5_onset   M10 M10_onset #>            P001    10080   7.00  0.72  0.43  0.89  12.3    02:30  84.7     11:00 #>            P002     9950   6.91  0.68  0.51  0.85  10.1    03:00  79.4     10:30

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: four stages validated epoch--epoch (0 / 76196 mismatches) Condor circadiaBase Python reference pipeline ActTrust recording. Default parameters acttrust_params() reproduce reference exactly.

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: zeitR/ ├── R/ │   ├── zeitR-package.R       # package-level docs │   ├── read_acttrust.R       # ActTrust file parser │   ├── read_actigraphy.R     # device-agnostic wrapper, zeitr_study │   ├── prepare.R             # temperature clamping, state columns │   ├── consistency.R         # timestamp quality checks │   ├── offwrist.R            # detect_offwrist_bimodal() │   ├── offwrist_refiner.R    # three-stage BimodalOffwristRefiner port │   ├── sleep_periods.R       # detect_sleep_crespo(), detect_naps_crespo() │   ├── cole_kripke.R         # score_epochs_cole_kripke() │   ├── waso.R                # compute_waso() │   ├── npcra.R               # compute_npcra() │   ├── study_summary.R       # study_summary() │   ├── params.R              # acttrust_params() device preset │   ├── pipeline.R            # run_pipeline(), run_pipeline_batch() │   └── utils.R               # label_states() + internal helpers ├── man/figures/ │   ├── logo.svg              # hex sticker │   └── favicon.svg           # favicon ├── vignettes/ │   ├── getting-started.Rmd │   ├── npcra.Rmd │   └── study-analysis.Rmd ├── tests/testthat/ ├── .github/workflows/ │   ├── R-CMD-check.yaml │   └── pkgdown.yaml ├── DESCRIPTION ├── NEWS.md └── zeitR.Rproj

title: ActTrust device parameter preset — acttrust_params
text: Returns named list device- study-specific parameter values used zeitR pipeline processing ActTrust recordings. Passing list (modified copy) run_pipeline() via params argument fully controls values detector stage receives, without touch individual function calls.

title: ActTrust device parameter preset — acttrust_params
code: acttrust_params()

title: ActTrust device parameter preset — acttrust_params
text: named list elements offwrist, sleep, nap, waso.

title: ActTrust device parameter preset — acttrust_params
text: list organised four named sections map directly four pipeline stages: offwrist Parameters detect_offwrist_bimodal(). values validated Condor circadiaBase pipeline using ActTrust hardware. refinement stage (.bimodal_refine_acttrust) device-specific currently validated ActTrust. sleep Parameters detect_sleep_crespo(). sleep_quantile (0.365) ActTrust CSPD wrapper value; original Crespo (2012) algorithm uses 1/3. nap Parameters detect_naps_crespo(), passed params argument. Equivalent .cspd_nap_params(). waso Parameters compute_waso(). adapt pipeline different device, copy list, modify relevant values, pass run_pipeline():
code: p <- acttrust_params() p$sleep$sleep_quantile <- 1/3   # use the original Crespo threshold result <- run_pipeline("recording.txt", params = p)

what: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
title: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
text: Scans recording tibble three classes timestamp problem:

title: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
code: check_consistency(x, gap_s = 120, datetime_col = "datetime")

title: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
text: x tibble returned read_acttrust() prepare_actigraphy(), containing datetime column. gap_s numeric(1). Gap threshold seconds. Intervals longer flagged. Default 120 (2 minutes). datetime_col character(1). Name datetime column. Default "datetime".

title: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
text: tibble one row per detected issue columns: row integer — row index x issue occurs. datetime POSIXct — timestamp row. issue character — one "gap", "backward_jump", "year_artefact". detail character — human-readable description. Returns zero-row tibble issues found.

title: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
text: Gaps — intervals consecutive epochs longer gap_s seconds. Backward jumps — timestamps go backwards time. Year artefacts — timestamps years 1970 2000, typically indicate firmware epoch-counter rollover bugs.

title: Check actigraphy timestamps for consistency issues — check_consistency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec    <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") issues <- check_consistency(rec) issues } # }

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
text: Computes standard non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis variables actigraphy recording, following Gonçalves et al. (2014) Van Someren et al. (1999). variables derived 24-hour average activity profile built hourly means (p = 24).

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
code: compute_npcra(   x,   epoch_s = NULL,   L5_hours = 5,   M10_hours = 10,   window_days = NULL )

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
text: x zeitr_recording returned read_actigraphy(), data frame / tibble least datetime activity columns. state column present, -wrist epochs (state == 4) excluded computing NPCRA variables. epoch_s numeric(1). Epoch duration seconds. NULL (default), estimated automatically median inter-epoch interval. L5_hours numeric(1). Width least-active window hours. Default 5. M10_hours numeric(1). Width -active window hours. Default 10. window_days numeric(1) NULL. supplied, recording split non-overlapping windows length (days) NPCRA variables computed window. window_start column added output. Partial final windows (shorter window_days) included flagged via lower n_days value. Default NULL computes single estimate full recording.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
text: tibble columns participant_id, window_start (window_days set), , IV, RA, L5, L5_onset, M10, M10_onset, n_days, n_epochs.

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
text: following variables computed: Interdaily stability — consistency 24 h rest-activity pattern across days (range 0–1; higher = stable). IV Intradaily variability — fragmentation rest-activity rhythm (>= 0; higher = fragmented). RA Relative amplitude — contrast active 10 h window (M10) least active 5 h window (L5) (range 0–1). L5 Mean activity least active 5 consecutive hours. L5_onset Clock time L5 window onset (hh:mm). M10 Mean activity active 10 consecutive hours. M10_onset Clock time M10 window onset (hh:mm).

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
text: Gonçalves, B. S. B., Adamowicz, T., Louzada, F. M., Moreno, C. R., & Araujo, J. F. (2014). fresh look use nonparametric analysis actimetry. Sleep Medicine Reviews, 20, 84–91. doi:10.1016/j.smrv.2014.06.002 Van Someren, E. J. W., Swaab, D. F., Colenda, C. C., Cohen, W., McCall, W. V., & Rosenquist, P. B. (1999). Bright light therapy: Improved sensitivity effects rest-activity rhythms Alzheimer patients application nonparametric methods. Chronobiology International, 16(4), 505–518. doi:10.3109/07420529908998724

title: Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis (NPCRA) — compute_npcra
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec   <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.txt")  # Single estimate over the full recording compute_npcra(rec)  # Per-fortnight estimates compute_npcra(rec, window_days = 14) } # }

what: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
text: Scores epoch within detected sleep periods wake sleep using score_epochs_cole_kripke(), computes per-night statistics. faithful port Python condor_pipeline detect_waso: night boundaries built .nights_df() (search_gap = FALSE path reference nights_df), night's ZCM scored Cole-Kripke, epoch-level state rebuilt zero (wake) base within-night WASO-wake epochs epochs outside detected nights scored wake.

title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
code: compute_waso(x, wake_thresh = 60L, search_gap = FALSE)

title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
text: x tibble returned detect_naps_crespo() (detect_sleep_crespo() nap detection skipped), containing columns datetime, ZCMn, state. wake_thresh integer(1). Minimum duration epochs wake bout required delimit new sleep period boundary. Default 60. search_gap logical(1). Reserved API compatibility reference pipeline. reference detect_waso always builds boundaries search_gap = FALSE, argument currently effect.

title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
text: list two elements: nights tibble one row per detected night/nap columns night, is_nap, bed_time, get_up_time, tbt, tst, waso, sol, soi, nw, eff. data input tibble x state sleep updated epoch-level Cole-Kripke wake/sleep scores.

title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
text: following metrics computed detected night / nap:

title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
text: Cole, R. J., Kripke, D. F., Gruen, W., Mullaney, D. J., & Gillin, J. C. (1992). Automatic sleep/wake identification wrist activity. Sleep, 15(5), 461–469. doi:10.1093/sleep/15.5.461

title: Compute WASO and nightly sleep statistics — compute_waso
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec    <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") prep   <- prepare_actigraphy(rec) prep   <- detect_offwrist_bimodal(prep) prep   <- detect_sleep_crespo(prep) prep   <- detect_naps_crespo(prep) result <- compute_waso(prep) result$nights } # }

what: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
text: Faithful port Python nap_wrapper: runs full CSPD model nap mode (detect_naps = TRUE) currently-awake epochs (state == 0) merges detected naps sleep state. Must run detect_sleep_crespo().

title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
code: detect_naps_crespo(x, epoch_h = NULL, params = .cspd_nap_params())

title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
text: x tibble returned detect_sleep_crespo(), containing columns datetime, activity, state. Nap detection runs wake (state == 0) subset , mirroring Python nap_bool mask. epoch_h numeric(1). Number epochs per hour. NULL (default), derived wake-subsequence epoch duration 3600 / duration. params CSPD nap configuration list (default .cspd_nap_params()), port nap_wrapper's parameter set.

title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
text: input tibble x state sleep columns updated. Nap epochs become state == 1 sleep == 1; -wrist (state == 4) existing main-sleep epochs preserved.

title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
text: Nap detection uses nap-mode MSP (high zero-proportion combined low adaptive-median activity, .crespo_nap_msp()) followed bed-time / get--time refiners main sleep detection, nap parameter set (.cspd_nap_params()) nap-specific minimum-length post-processing. Detected naps written state == 1 (merged "sleep"), matching nap_wrapper, assigns state[wake] = 1 - refined_output (.e. naps distinct state).

title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
text: Crespo, C., Aboy, M., Fernández, J. R., & Mojón, . (2012). Automatic identification activity-rest periods based actigraphy. Journal Medical Biological Engineering, 32(4), 249–256. doi:10.5405/jmbe.1033

title: Detect secondary sleep periods (naps) using the Crespo nap algorithm — detect_naps_crespo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec  <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") prep <- prepare_actigraphy(rec) prep <- detect_offwrist_bimodal(prep) prep <- detect_sleep_crespo(prep) prep <- detect_naps_crespo(prep) } # }

what: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
text: Detects periods actigraph worn using bimodal algorithm developed Condor Instruments. algorithm proceeds three stages:

title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
code: detect_offwrist_bimodal(   x,   hws = 10L,   activity_quantile = 0.15,   min_norm_activity = 0.015,   nbins = 100L,   min_offwrist_length = 10L,   min_temp_threshold = 0.35 )

title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
text: x tibble returned prepare_actigraphy(), containing columns datetime, activity (PIM), int_temp, ext_temp, state, offwrist. hws integer(1). Half-window size (epochs) rolling feature extraction. Default 10 (matching Python pipeline). activity_quantile numeric(1). Quantile used define "low activity". Default 0.15. min_norm_activity numeric(1). Minimum normalised activity threshold low-activity cutoff clamped. Default 0.015. nbins integer(1). Number histogram bins used fitting GMM threshold. Default 100. min_offwrist_length integer(1). Minimum number consecutive -wrist epochs required retain detected period. Shorter runs discarded. Default 10. min_temp_threshold numeric(1). Minimum normalised temperature threshold; fitted GMM threshold clamped value falls . Default 0.35.

title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
text: input tibble x state offwrist columns updated. -wrist epochs state == 4 offwrist == 0.25.

title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
text: Feature extraction — rolling median PIM activity rolling signal/variance internal temperature computed symmetric window half-width hws epochs. Bimodal temperature threshold — among epochs low activity median (activity_quantile quantile normalised activity), 2-component Gaussian Mixture Model fitted normalised temperature distribution. threshold two components taken minimum GMM density two means (minimum smoothed histogram lower). Ashman's D computed bimodality quality metric. Initial classification & refinement — epochs simultaneously exhibiting low activity median low temperature marked -wrist. Short spurious -wrist runs shorter min_offwrist_length epochs removed. -wrist epochs encoded state == 4 (matching Python pipeline convention). offwrist column set 0.25 -wrist epochs actogram overlay plotting.

title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
text: bimodal -wrist algorithm developed Julius . P. P. de Paula Condor Instruments (2023). published peer-reviewed literature source code available circadiaBase pipeline repository. Ashman D statistic described : Ashman, K. M., Bird, C. M., & Zepf, S. E. (1994). Detecting bimodality astronomical datasets. Astronomical Journal, 108, 2348. doi:10.1086/117248

title: Off-wrist detection using the Condor bimodal activity/temperature model — detect_offwrist_bimodal
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec  <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") prep <- prepare_actigraphy(rec) prep <- detect_offwrist_bimodal(prep) sum(prep$state == 4)  # number of off-wrist epochs } # }

what: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
title: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
text: Identifies main sleep period(s) actigraphy recording using algorithm described Crespo et al. (2012). method applies adaptive median filter activity signal, mitigates spuriously long zero runs, thresholds result quantile filtered signal. Morphological closing opening operations used smooth binary sleep/wake estimate.

title: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
code: detect_sleep_crespo(   x,   epoch_h = NULL,   median_filter_h = 8,   pad_h = 1,   sleep_quantile = 0.365,   morph_size = 61L,   consec_zeros_thr = 15L,   awake_zeros_thr = 2L,   sleep_zeros_thr = 30L,   zero_mitigation_q = 0.33,   min_short_window_thr = 1,   refine = TRUE,   condition = 0L )

title: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
text: x tibble returned detect_offwrist_bimodal() (prepare_actigraphy() -wrist detection skipped), containing columns datetime, activity, state. detector runs -wrist subset (state != 4) , mirroring Python cspd_wrapper. epoch_h numeric(1). Number epochs per hour. NULL (default), derived epoch duration (mode -wrist inter-epoch interval), 3600 / duration. median_filter_h numeric(1). Length preprocessing median filter window hours. Default 8. pad_h numeric(1). Padding length hours added adaptive median filter. Default 1. sleep_quantile numeric(1). Quantile filtered activity used MSP sleep/wake threshold. Default 0.365 (ActTrust CSPD value used cspd_wrapper; standalone Crespo algorithm uses 1/3). morph_size integer(1). Size structuring element used morphological closing/opening. Default 61 epochs. consec_zeros_thr integer(1). Runs zeros longer threshold treated invalid (zero mitigation). Default 15. awake_zeros_thr integer(1). Threshold consecutive zeros within wake periods. Default 2. sleep_zeros_thr integer(1). Threshold consecutive zeros within sleep periods. Default 30. zero_mitigation_q numeric(1). Quantile activity used determine mitigation level invalid zero runs. Default 0.33. min_short_window_thr numeric(1). Minimum value adaptive median threshold; fitted quantile falls , threshold clamped . Default 1.0. refine logical(1). TRUE (default), MSP detection refined final sleep periods CSPD bed-time / get--time refiners (.cspd_refine_periods), reproducing Python refined_output. FALSE, raw MSP detection used directly. condition integer(1). Initial condition flag (default 0). MSP stage bumps 2 activity-median threshold clamps min_short_window_thr; refiner uses effective condition. Affects refine = TRUE.

title: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
text: input tibble x state sleep columns updated. Sleep epochs state == 1 sleep == 1; -wrist epochs (state == 4) preserved excluded sleep column. refine = TRUE sleep epochs delimit refined sleep PERIODS (Python refined_output); per-epoch wake/sleep within scored later compute_waso().

title: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
text: Crespo, C., Aboy, M., Fernández, J. R., & Mojón, . (2012). Automatic identification activity-rest periods based actigraphy. Journal Medical Biological Engineering, 32(4), 249–256. doi:10.5405/jmbe.1033

title: Detect main sleep periods using the Crespo algorithm — detect_sleep_crespo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec  <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") prep <- prepare_actigraphy(rec) prep <- detect_offwrist_bimodal(prep) prep <- detect_sleep_crespo(prep) } # }

what: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
title: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
text: Converts integer state column produced zeitR pipeline human-readable factor. Useful display, plotting, export — internal state column always stays integer preserve Python reference parity.

title: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
code: label_states(x)

title: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
text: x integer (numeric) vector epoch states, found result$data$state.

title: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
text: ordered factor levels c("wake", "sleep", "nap", "-wrist"), length x.

title: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
text: value table silently converted NA.

title: Convert integer epoch states to a labelled factor — label_states
code: label_states(c(0L, 1L, 0L, 4L, 1L, 7L)) #>         0         1         0         4         1         7  #>      wake     sleep      wake off-wrist     sleep       nap  #> Levels: wake < sleep < nap < off-wrist # [1] wake  sleep wake  off-wrist sleep nap # Levels: wake < sleep < nap < off-wrist  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_pipeline("recordings/P001.txt") result$data$state_label <- label_states(result$data$state) } # }

what: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
title: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
text: Transforms output read_acttrust() (reader returns compatible tibble) working data frame expected detection functions. Specifically:

title: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
code: prepare_actigraphy(x)

title: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
text: x tibble returned read_acttrust(), containing minimum int_temp ext_temp columns.

title: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
text: tibble dimensions x additional updated columns: state, offwrist, sleep, int_temp_, ext_temp_.

title: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
text: Clamps int_temp ext_temp physiological range [0, 42] °C. Adds min-max scaled temperature columns int_temp_ ext_temp_ (range [0, 1]) plotting. Ensures state, offwrist, sleep columns present initialised 0. original tibble never modified; copy returned.

title: Prepare a raw actigraphy tibble for analysis — prepare_actigraphy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec  <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") prep <- prepare_actigraphy(rec) } # }

what: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
title: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
text: device-agnostic wrapper reads raw actigraphy file returns zeitr_recording object $epochs (tidy tibble) $metadata (named list device recording information).

title: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
code: read_actigraphy(path, device = "acttrust", tz = "UTC", ...)

title: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
text: path character(1). Path raw actigraphy file. device character(1). Device type. One "acttrust" (default). Additional devices added future versions. tz character(1). Recording time zone passed underlying reader. Default "UTC". ... Additional arguments forwarded device-specific reader (e.g. encoding read_acttrust()).

title: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
text: zeitr_recording S3 object — named list : $epochs tibble one row per epoch columns datetime, activity, int_temp, ext_temp, ZCMn, state, offwrist, sleep. $metadata named list subject, device_id, device_model, firmware_version, interval_s, source_file, participant_id (derived filename stem).

title: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
text: Currently supported devices: "acttrust" — Condor Instruments ActTrust / ActTrust2 (.txt)

title: Read an actigraphy file into a zeitr_recording object — read_actigraphy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.txt") rec$epochs rec$metadata } # }

what: Read all actigraphy files in a directory — read_actigraphy_dir
title: Read all actigraphy files in a directory — read_actigraphy_dir
text: Applies read_actigraphy() every file matching pattern folder. Returns zeitr_study object — named list zeitr_recording objects, one per file. Files fail parse skipped warning.

title: Read all actigraphy files in a directory — read_actigraphy_dir
code: read_actigraphy_dir(   folder,   device = "acttrust",   pattern = "*.txt",   tz = "UTC",   ... )

title: Read all actigraphy files in a directory — read_actigraphy_dir
text: folder character(1). Path directory containing actigraphy files. device character(1). Device type passed read_actigraphy(). Default "acttrust". pattern character(1). Glob pattern file discovery. Default "*.txt". tz character(1). Recording time zone. Default "UTC". ... Additional arguments forwarded read_actigraphy().

title: Read all actigraphy files in a directory — read_actigraphy_dir
text: zeitr_study S3 object — named list zeitr_recording objects. Names participant IDs (filename stems).

title: Read all actigraphy files in a directory — read_actigraphy_dir
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ study <- read_actigraphy_dir("recordings/", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") study_summary(study) } # }

what: Read a Condor Instruments ActTrust actigraphy file — read_acttrust
title: Read a Condor Instruments ActTrust actigraphy file — read_acttrust
text: Parses Condor ActTrust .txt export tidy tibble. file format consists variable-length key-value header block followed semicolon-delimited epoch rows. header ends line beginning DATE/TIME.

title: Read a Condor Instruments ActTrust actigraphy file — read_acttrust
code: read_acttrust(path, tz = "UTC", encoding = "latin1")

title: Read a Condor Instruments ActTrust actigraphy file — read_acttrust
text: path character(1) fs::path. Path ActTrust .txt file. tz character(1). Time zone string passed lubridate::parse_date_time(). Defaults "UTC". Set local recording time zone correct circadian alignment. encoding character(1). File encoding. Defaults "latin1", matches Condor's default export encoding.

title: Read a Condor Instruments ActTrust actigraphy file — read_acttrust
text: tibble one row per epoch following columns: datetime POSIXct — epoch timestamp. activity double — PIM activity count. int_temp double — internal (-body) temperature, °C. ext_temp double — external (ambient) temperature, °C. NA unavailable. ZCMn double — normalised zero-crossing mode count. NA unavailable. state double — state column, initialised 0. offwrist double — -wrist indicator, initialised 0. sleep double — sleep indicator, initialised 0. tibble carries "zeitr_recording" class metadata attribute (named list subject, device_id, device_model, firmware_version, interval_s, source_file).

title: Read a Condor Instruments ActTrust actigraphy file — read_acttrust
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") rec attr(rec, "metadata") } # }

what: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
title: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
text: Orchestrates complete pipeline single ActTrust recording:

title: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
code: run_pipeline(   path,   tz = "UTC",   gap_s = 120,   params = acttrust_params(),   offwrist_args = list(),   sleep_args = list(),   nap_args = list(),   quiet = FALSE )

title: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
text: path character(1). Path ActTrust .txt file. tz character(1). Recording time zone. Passed read_acttrust(). Default "UTC". gap_s numeric(1). Gap threshold (seconds) check_consistency(). Default 120. params Device parameter preset, returned acttrust_params(). supplied, values params used defaults detector stage, explicit offwrist_args, sleep_args, nap_args taking precedence. Defaults acttrust_params(). offwrist_args list. Additional arguments passed detect_offwrist_bimodal(). Default empty list. sleep_args list. Additional arguments passed detect_sleep_crespo(). Default empty list. nap_args list. Additional arguments passed detect_naps_crespo(). Default empty list. quiet logical(1). TRUE, suppresses timestamp-issue warning emitted check_consistency() finds problems. Useful batch testing contexts warning expected. Default FALSE.

title: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
text: zeitr_result S3 object — named list : subject_id character — derived input filename stem. source_file character — absolute path input file. data tibble — final epoch-level data frame state columns populated. nights tibble — per-night sleep statistics. issues tibble — timestamp consistency issues (0 rows none). metadata list — device subject metadata file header.

title: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
text: Read — read_acttrust() Consistency check — check_consistency() Prepare — prepare_actigraphy() -wrist detection — detect_offwrist_bimodal() Main sleep period detection — detect_sleep_crespo() Nap detection — detect_naps_crespo() WASO + nightly statistics — compute_waso()

title: Run the full actigraphy sleep analysis pipeline — run_pipeline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_pipeline("recordings/P001.txt", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") result$nights result$data } # }

what: Run the pipeline on all files in a directory — run_pipeline_batch
title: Run the pipeline on all files in a directory — run_pipeline_batch
text: Applies run_pipeline() every file matching pattern folder, returning list zeitr_result objects. Files fail skipped warning.

title: Run the pipeline on all files in a directory — run_pipeline_batch
code: run_pipeline_batch(folder, pattern = "*.txt", ...)

title: Run the pipeline on all files in a directory — run_pipeline_batch
text: folder character(1). Path directory containing ActTrust files. pattern character(1). Glob pattern file discovery. Default "*.txt". ... Additional arguments forwarded run_pipeline().

title: Run the pipeline on all files in a directory — run_pipeline_batch
text: named list zeitr_result objects, one per successfully processed file. Names file stem (subject IDs).

title: Run the pipeline on all files in a directory — run_pipeline_batch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_pipeline_batch("recordings/", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") lapply(results, function(r) r$nights) } # }

what: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
title: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
text: Applies Cole-Kripke algorithm vector zero-crossing mode (ZCM) activity counts, scoring epoch wake (1) sleep (0) using weighted sum activity surrounding window.

title: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
text: zcm numeric vector ZCM activity counts, one value per epoch. P numeric(1). Scaling factor. Default 0.000464 (Cole et al., 1992). weights_before numeric(9). Weights applied 9 epochs current epoch. Defaults values Cole et al. (1992), Table 2. weights_after numeric(8). Weights applied 8 epochs current epoch. Defaults values Cole et al. (1992), Table 2.

title: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
text: integer vector length zcm, 1 indicating wake 0 indicating sleep.

title: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
text: epoch's score computed : $$D_i = P \sum_{j=1}^{9} W_j^{-} \cdot A_{-j} +             P \sum_{j=1}^{8} W_j^{+} \cdot A_{+j}$$ \(A_i\) ZCM count epoch \(\), \(W^{-}\) \(W^{+}\) weight vectors Cole et al. (1992), \(P = 0.000464\). Epochs \(D_i \ge 1\) scored wake.

title: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
text: Cole, R. J., Kripke, D. F., Gruen, W., Mullaney, D. J., & Gillin, J. C. (1992). Automatic sleep/wake identification wrist activity. Sleep, 15(5), 461–469. doi:10.1093/sleep/15.5.461

title: Score actigraphy epochs as wake or sleep using the Cole-Kripke algorithm — score_epochs_cole_kripke
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec    <- read_acttrust("recordings/P001.txt") scores <- score_epochs_cole_kripke(rec$ZCMn) table(scores)  # 0 = sleep, 1 = wake } # }

what: Summarise a zeitr_study across participants — study_summary
title: Summarise a zeitr_study across participants — study_summary
text: Computes per-participant summary statistics zeitr_study object (returned read_actigraphy_dir()). recording, function computes NPCRA variables (, IV, RA, L5, M10) basic recording quality metrics.

title: Summarise a zeitr_study across participants — study_summary
code: study_summary(study, epoch_s = NULL, L5_hours = 5, M10_hours = 10)

title: Summarise a zeitr_study across participants — study_summary
text: study zeitr_study object returned read_actigraphy_dir(), named list zeitr_recording objects. epoch_s numeric(1). Epoch duration seconds. NULL (default), estimated separately recording. L5_hours numeric(1). Width L5 window hours. Default 5. M10_hours numeric(1). Width M10 window hours. Default 10.

title: Summarise a zeitr_study across participants — study_summary
text: tibble one row per participant columns: participant_id Participant identifier (filename stem). n_epochs Total number epochs recording. n_days Recording duration days. start POSIXct — first epoch timestamp. end POSIXct — last epoch timestamp. Interdaily stability. IV Intradaily variability. RA Relative amplitude. L5 Mean activity least active 5 h window. L5_onset Clock time L5 midpoint. M10 Mean activity active 10 h window. M10_onset Clock time M10 midpoint.

title: Summarise a zeitr_study across participants — study_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ study <- read_actigraphy_dir("recordings/", tz = "America/Sao_Paulo") study_summary(study) } # }

what: zeitR: Actigraphy Data Parsing and Analysis for R — zeitR-package
title: zeitR: Actigraphy Data Parsing and Analysis for R — zeitR-package
text: Provides tools importing, parsing, analysing raw actigraphy data. Supports common device file formats, computes standard rest-activity variables (, IV, RA, L5, M10), assembles tidy data frames ready downstream chronobiological analysis. Designed complement slumbR Circadia Lab ecosystem.

title: zeitR: Actigraphy Data Parsing and Analysis for R — zeitR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

title: zeitR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: Full actigraphy pipeline validated epoch--epoch (0 / 76,196 mismatches) Condor circadiaBase Python reference ActTrust recording: detect_offwrist_bimodal(), detect_sleep_crespo(), detect_naps_crespo() (faithful nap_wrapper port), compute_waso(). run_pipeline() gains params argument (default acttrust_params()); device-specific defaults now consolidated one place can overridden without touching individual detector calls. wake_thresh removed run_pipeline() signature — now lives params$waso$wake_thresh. run_pipeline() gains quiet argument suppress timestamp-issue warning (useful batch testing contexts).

title: zeitR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: acttrust_params() — exported device parameter preset consolidating ActTrust-specific defaults across -wrist, sleep, nap, WASO stages. Copy modify adapt pipeline devices. label_states() — converts integer state column human-readable ordered factor ("wake", "sleep", "nap", "-wrist").

title: zeitR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: End--end pipeline parity regression test (test-pipeline-parity.R): epoch-level state, per-layer counts, nightly statistics locked python_output.csv python_nights.csv. CSPD refiner parity tests (test-cspd-refiner-parity.R): stage-1 peak-valley length filter, stage-2 sleep-gap separation, full .cspd_refine_periods output, bedtime/getuptime indices Python intermediates. Sleep Crespo wiring test (test-sleep-crespo-wiring.R): isolates detect_sleep_crespo(refine = TRUE) cspd_refined_output.csv. WASO parity tests (test-waso-parity.R): .nights_df boundaries, per-night statistics, within-night epoch agreement boundary-matched nights.

title: zeitR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: Full package scaffold: read_acttrust(), read_actigraphy(), read_actigraphy_dir(), prepare_actigraphy(), check_consistency(), score_epochs_cole_kripke(), compute_npcra(), study_summary(), run_pipeline_batch(). Three vignettes: getting started, NPCRA, study-level analysis. pkgdown site Bootstrap 5 Circadia Lab branding.
